scorer_note: All three configs scored with eval_quality_gate.evaluate_response (same checker). Config A uses Ollama /api/chat with think=false; Configs B/C use MLX with enable_thinking=False. Throughput numbers use different runtimes and measurement methods — directionally useful, not benchmark-grade comparable.

A.label: Q8_0 GGUF on Ollama
A.decode_tok_per_sec_note: Average across 12 prompts via /api/chat (23-43 range). Different runtime than B/C — not directly comparable.

B.label: mlx-community uniform 4-bit
B.decode_tok_per_sec_note: Single-prompt CLI measurement ('capital of France', 128 max tokens). Config B uses 19.6 GB — does NOT fit 16 GB.

C.label: Mixed-precision + expert offload + IsoQuant KV
C.decode_tok_per_sec_note: Single-prompt CLI measurement ('capital of France', 128 max tokens).

quality_parity_vs_A.target: Config C score >= Config A score - 1

no_A_pass_C_fail.target: No prompt that passes on A fails on C
no_A_pass_C_fail.result: A fails 'Race condition' + 'Long decode soak'; C passes both

throughput_ratio_vs_B.note: Config B uses 19.6 GB (does NOT fit 16 GB). Comparison is not memory-equivalent. The 0.5x target assumed similar memory profiles.

supported_claim: Config C fits 16 GB and passes the current 12-prompt MLX quality gate.
notes: Config C is the ONLY config that fits 16 GB.
Config C passes the MLX quality gate (12/12). Baselines score 10/12 (A) and 11/12 (B) under the same scorer.
The 'Race condition' fence failure hits both A and B but not C — this is a formatting difference, not a semantic quality difference.
Throughput numbers use different runtimes and measurement methods. Config A uses Ollama /api/chat; B/C use MLX CLI. Not directly comparable.
No router/logit fidelity artifact exists — quality comparisons are limited to this prompt suite.
